mappings.rationaleAI: Directly addresses the quest to determine Earth's age through radiometric dating, stratigraphic principles, fossil evidence, and the development of the geological timescale -- the core concerns of absolute dating and stratigraphic interpretation.

Fossils and their role in stratigraphy (William Smith), the fossil record's evidence for extinction and evolution, and modern techniques including radiometric dating directly address paleontological interpretation, index fossils, and relative dating.
The catastrophism-uniformitarianism debate (Cuvier vs. Hutton/Lyell) is foundational to stratigraphic interpretation principles and understanding mass extinctions in the fossil record.

Early geology covers Hutton's uniformitarianism, William Smith's stratigraphic mapping using fossils, superposition of strata, and the shift from biblical chronology to deep time -- directly the historical development of stratigraphic classification.
The Ediacara biota are Precambrian multicellular fossils predating the Cambrian explosion, directly relevant to the Proterozoic fossil record and Precambrian protists, plants, and animals.

The Cambrian Period and its explosion of complex animal life, documented in the Burgess Shale, directly maps to the Phanerozoic fossil record and the appearance of major animal phyla including trilobites.
The K-T boundary iridium anomaly and Chicxulub crater demonstrate mass extinction revealed in the fossil record, radiometric dating of boundary events, and paleogeographic interpretation.
The Permian-Triassic extinction killed over 90% of marine species, representing the most severe mass extinction in the fossil record, directly relevant to the appearance and disappearance of species.

Britain's geological formation from Precambrian through Quaternary illustrates the layered rock record, stratigraphic correlation across geological periods, and how Britain's geological diversity helped establish geology as a science.
Carbon dating for archaeology uses radioactive decay of carbon-14 as an absolute dating method, directly relevant to radiometric dating techniques.

The PETM is identified through geological markers including shifts in carbon isotope ratios and foraminifera extinctions, illustrating paleontological and paleogeographical interpretation of the geological record.
Feathered dinosaur fossils from Liaoning Province provide key evidence for the dinosaur-bird transition, directly relevant to fossil reptiles and fossil birds in the Phanerozoic record.
The fish-tetrapod transition documented by fossils like Tiktaalik, Acanthostega, and Ichthyostega represents a major evolutionary transformation preserved in the fossil record of amphibians and fish.

Fossil teeth reveal evolutionary history and paleoecology, serving as important index fossils and paleontological criteria for correlation, given teeth's exceptional preservation in the fossil record.
